summary:
During early developmental phases, specifically at the tooth bud phase when it forms into the enamel organ 'bell', the process begins that can lead to multiple root formations through specific patterns set by epithelial structures within this structure. Cell rests are important but only maintain these formed roots; their initiation comes from prior structural divisions occurring around embryonic weeks four-five post-conception. \boxed{C}
The primary mechanism leading to multifurcated roots involves segmentation processes initiated between week five and seven after conception while still part of the developing tooth germ before any definitive pulp chamber has been established – hence pinpointing options relating directly back here most precisely answers our query regarding etiology behind extra-root canal occurrences seen clinically across various ages including adults who retain deciduous successors too. 

Key Points Summarized:
- Initial pattern setting happens via segmented regions defined pre-bell-stage
- Multiple roots form based off those segments created initially

This summary captures essential aspects without extraneous details focusing strictly upon fundamental causes linked closely enough temporally & anatomically speaking towards observed outcomes noted historically amongst patients presenting complex dentitions needing endodontic intervention among other potential complications arising secondary thereto depending also heavily uppon individual patient factors alongside general health status etc...
